title: Home Assistant Integration
summary: Home Assistant integration via WebSocket API, covering state_changed event subscriptions, 4 LLM-callable HA tools (get_state, call_service, get_history, list_entities), event filtering, blocked service domains for safety, and persistent notification delivery.
key_concepts: state_changed event subscription for real-time updates
4 LLM-callable HA tools: get_state, call_service, get_history, list_entities
Event filtering to avoid LLM overload from noisy sensors
Blocked service domains for safety (e.g. alarm_control_panel)
Persistent notifications for HA frontend delivery
Entity domain filtering (lights, switches, sensors, etc.)

env_vars.name: HA_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Long-lived access token from HA Profile > Long-Lived Access Tokens

env_vars.name: HA_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated entity domains the LLM may control (e.g. light,switch,climate)

env_vars.name: HA_BLOCKED_DOMAINS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated service domains blocked from LLM call_service calls

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Home Assistant WebSocket listener

config_keys.description: Entity domains LLM may control (empty = all non-blocked)
Service domains blocked from LLM calls (default: [alarm_control_panel])
Entity ID patterns to subscribe to for state_changed events
Deliver responses as HA persistent notifications (default: false)

code_examples.title: Home Assistant config
code_examples.code:
homeassistant:
  url: "http://homeassistant.local:8123"
  token: "${HA_TOKEN}"
  allowed_domains:
    - light
    - switch
    - climate
    - media_player
  blocked_domains:
    - alarm_control_panel
    - lock

code_examples.title: Get long-lived access token
code_examples.code:
HA Frontend > Profile (bottom left) > Long-Lived Access Tokens > Create Token
Name: hermes
Copy the token (shown only once)

code_examples.title: LLM-callable HA tools
code_examples.code:
get_state(entity_id)           - Get current state of any entity
call_service(domain, service, data) - Call any HA service (e.g. light.turn_on)
get_history(entity_id, hours)  - Get state history for the past N hours
list_entities(domain)          - List all entities in a domain

code_examples.title: Event filter for specific entities
code_examples.code:
homeassistant:
  event_filter:
    - "binary_sensor.front_door*"
    - "sensor.living_room_temperature"
  # Only state_changed events matching these patterns trigger LLM

gotchas: Long-lived access tokens grant full HA access; restrict via blocked_domains to prevent LLM from controlling safety-critical systems
Without event_filter, state_changed events from ALL entities flood the gateway; noisy sensors (motion, power monitors) will trigger LLM on every update
call_service blocked_domains blocks the domain entirely; individual service blocking is not supported
The 4 HA tools are only available when the homeassistant integration is enabled; they do not appear for other gateway platforms
HA WebSocket connection drops on HA restart; hermes auto-reconnects but brief gaps occur
persistent_notifications require the notify.persistent_notification service in HA which is built-in
full_text:
## Home Assistant

Connect Hermes to [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) via the WebSocket API.

### Setup

#### 1. Create a long-lived access token

In the HA frontend:
1. Click your profile (bottom left).
2. Scroll to **Long-Lived Access Tokens**.
3. Click **Create Token**, name it "hermes", copy it (shown only once).

#### 2. Config

```yaml
homeassistant:
  url: "http://homeassistant.local:8123"
  token: "${HA_TOKEN}"
  allowed_domains:
    - light
    - switch
    - climate
    - media_player
  blocked_domains:
    - alarm_control_panel
    - lock
```

### LLM tools

When the Home Assistant integration is active, the LLM gets 4 tools:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `get_state(entity_id)` | Get current state and attributes of any entity |
| `call_service(domain, service, data)` | Call any HA service (e.g. `light.turn_on`) |
| `get_history(entity_id, hours)` | Get state history for the past N hours |
| `list_entities(domain)` | List all entities in a domain |

Example: "Turn on the kitchen lights" → LLM calls `call_service("light", "turn_on", {"entity_id": "light.kitchen"})`

### Safety: blocked domains

Certain service domains are blocked from LLM calls to prevent accidents:

```yaml
blocked_domains:
  - alarm_control_panel  # default
  - lock                 # add if desired
```

The LLM cannot call services in blocked domains regardless of what the user asks.

### Event filtering

Without a filter, every state_changed event from every entity triggers the LLM. Use event_filter to restrict:

```yaml
homeassistant:
  event_filter:
    - "binary_sensor.front_door*"
    - "sensor.living_room_temperature"
```

Only state_changed events from matching entities will trigger the gateway.

### Persistent notifications

Deliver Hermes responses as HA persistent notifications (visible in the HA frontend):

```yaml
homeassistant:
  persistent_notifications: true
```

### Natural language control

With the integration active, you can send natural language commands via any Hermes-connected platform:
- "What's the temperature in the living room?"
- "Turn off all lights in the bedroom"
- "Show me the front door sensor history for the last 4 hours"

Hermes uses the HA tools to fulfill these requests.
